response.justification: The candidate is a skilled polyglot software engineer with strong testing and craftsmanship practices, but is fundamentally misaligned for this mid-senior C++ industrial systems role. Only 6 months of C++ experience (2014-2015 at National Instruments) versus the required 8-12 years; the career trajectory has moved away from systems programming into web/TypeScript/Node.js. Zero industrial automation, IoT, or industrial cybersecurity domain knowledge (SDL, IEC 62443 standards); no experience with required protocols or multi-threaded real-time systems. While capable of learning, the domain gaps and C++ deficit are too large for a mid-senior position.
strengths: Deep software engineering fundamentals emphasizing TDD, code quality, and Agile craftsmanship across multiple organizations
Demonstrated ability to rapidly learn and contribute across diverse technical stacks and problem domains
Leadership through mentoring, technical workshops, open-source contributions, and guidance on testing/CI practices
concerns: Only 6 months of C++ experience in 2014-2015 (LabWindows IDE work); 10+ years of recent work in TypeScript/Node.js/web stack
Zero background in industrial automation, IoT, cybersecurity standards (SDL, IEC 62443), or real-time/multi-threaded systems design
No experience with required industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, DNP 3.0, OPC UA) or message-broker architectures
